decks.notes: This Boros Aggro deck focuses on a fast and aggressive strategy, utilizing cards like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Guide of Souls to quickly deal damage to the opponent. Voice of Victory and Arena of Glory provide additional support and buffs for the creatures on the battlefield. The deck also includes removal spells like Galvanic Discharge and Thraben Charm to control the board. With a mix of creatures, spells, and lands to support its aggressive gameplay, this deck aims to overwhelm the opponent and secure a swift victory.

The deck focuses on quickly filling the graveyard with cards like Faithless Looting and Burning Inquiry to trigger the mechanics of Vengevine, Hollow One, and Detective's Phoenix. Marauding Mako helps to keep up the pressure while cards like Lightning Bolt offer removal options. Utilizing key lands like Arena of Glory and Commercial District, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with aggressive creatures and burn spells, ultimately securing a fast victory in a 1 vs 1 Modern format.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on fast and aggressive gameplay, utilizing cards like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Ocelot Pride to apply pressure early on. The deck also includes powerful cards such as Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Seasoned Pyromancer for additional threats and card advantage. With support cards like Guide of Souls and Galvanic Discharge, this deck aims to overwhelm the opponent quickly and efficiently. The inclusion of cards like Arena of Glory and Goblin Bombardment provide additional utility and reach to close out games swiftly.

This UR Control deck focuses on controlling the game through a combination of removal spells, counterspells, and card advantage. The deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan by removing threats and countering key spells, while also generating card advantage through cards like Snapcaster Mage and Expressive Iteration. The deck also utilizes a small amount of proactive threats to close out the game efficiently once the opponent's resources have been depleted. Overall, the deck's key concept is to maintain control of the game and outvalue the opponent through careful decision-making and resource management.

The deck focuses on assembling the Allosaurus Combo by using cards like Eldritch Evolution and Neoform to quickly find Allosaurus Rider and generate mana with Nourishing Shoal. The combo allows for explosive plays, powering out big threats like Griselbrand, Atraxa, and Ghalta while controlling the board with Pact of Negation and Veil of Summer. Consign to Memory and Planar Genesis provide additional utility and card advantage to help the deck reach its end game. The deck aims to overwhelm opponents with powerful creatures and versatile combo pieces.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on quickly overwhelming the opponent with a combination of low-cost aggressive creatures and burn spells. Using cards like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Seasoned Pyromancer, along with versatile removal like Galvanic Discharge and Voice of Victory, the deck aims to put pressure on the opponent early and maintain it throughout the game. With a mix of creature-based aggression and direct damage, the deck aims to close out games quickly before the opponent can stabilize.

This deck focuses on quickly putting pressure on the opponent with aggressive creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Phelia, Exuberant Shepherd. It also disrupts the opponent's game plan with cards like Quantum Riddler and Solitude, while controlling the board with cards like Wrath of the Skies and Prismatic Ending. The deck also has some flicker effects like Ephemerate to reuse powerful enter-the-battlefield abilities, and utilizes lands like Arid Mesa and Flooded Strand to fix mana and thin the deck. It aims to close out games quickly while disrupting the opponent's strategy.

This deck focuses on creating a high storm count by casting a large number of spells in one turn. The key cards are Ruby Medallion and Ral, Monsoon Mage to reduce the cost of spells and generate value. Past in Flames allows you to recast spells from your graveyard, while Glimpse the Impossible and Reckless Impulse help you dig through your deck to find your combo pieces. The end goal is to cast a lethal Grapeshot after generating a high storm count, using cards like Desperate Ritual and Manamorphose to create mana and draw cards.

This Aggro - Affinity deck focuses on quickly putting artifacts onto the battlefield to power up key creatures and generate value through recurring spells and abilities. It aims to overwhelm the opponent with a combination of aggressive creatures like Cori-Steel Cutter and Emry, Lurker of the Loch, along with supporting artifacts like Mishra's Bauble and Mox Opal. The deck also includes disruption through counterspells like Metallic Rebuke and removal like Lightning Bolt and Unholy Heat. Additionally, it utilizes land-based synergy with Urza's Saga and color fixing to ensure consistent, fast plays.

This deck aims to quickly fill the graveyard with big creatures like Griselbrand and Atraxa, Grand Unifier, then use instant spells like Goryo's Vengeance and Ephemerate to bring them back to the battlefield for a powerful combo. It also includes disruptive cards like Thoughtseize and Consign to Memory to control the opponent's plays. With a focus on reanimating creatures at instant speed, as well as leveraging cards that provide additional value when they leave the battlefield, this deck can quickly overwhelm opponents with its powerful creatures and tricky plays.

The deck focuses on using disruptive spells like Fatal Push and Thoughtseize to control the opponent's board while utilizing creatures like Flickerwisp and Solitude to apply pressure. Phelia, Exuberant Shepherd and Overlord of the Balemurk provide powerful threats that can quickly close out the game. The deck also includes cards like Teferi, Time Raveler and Ephemerate to protect its own creatures and disrupt the opponent's game plan. The strategy centers around disrupting the opponent's plans while deploying efficient threats to win the game quickly.

The main concept of this Boros Aggro deck is to overwhelm the opponent with a combination of aggressive creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Guide of Souls, supported by powerful spells like Goblin Bombardment and Ajani, Nacatl Pariah. The deck also utilizes cards like Blood Moon and Ranger-Captain of Eos to disrupt the opponent's game plan, while Seasoned Pyromancer provides card advantage. The inclusion of Phlage, Titan of Fire's Fury as a finisher adds a strong late-game threat. Overall, the deck aims to apply constant pressure and quickly close out the game.

This deck focuses on applying early pressure with aggressive creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Phelia, Exuberant Shepherd, while disrupting the opponent's game plan with cards like Teferi, Time Raveler and Prismatic Ending. The deck also utilizes powerful removal spells like Solitude and Galvanic Discharge to control the board. Consign to Memory and Fable of the Mirror-Breaker provide card advantage and flexibility. Overall, the deck aims to quickly close out the game by overwhelming the opponent with a mix of aggro threats and disruptive elements.

The deck focuses on quickly playing artifacts and utilizing them for aggressive plays, while also disrupting the opponent with cards like Tormod's Crypt and Metallic Rebuke. Emry, Lurker of the Loch is used to recur artifacts and provide card advantage. Urza's Saga helps to search for key artifacts while Pinnacle Emissary and Kappa Cannoneer provide additional threats. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a mix of fast creatures and disruptive elements, ultimately trying to win the game through a combination of aggression and resource denial.

This deck focuses on aggressive gameplay with a mix of creatures and spells to quickly overwhelm the opponent. It utilizes cards like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Territorial Kavu to apply pressure early on, while Leyline of Binding and Leyline of the Guildpact help control the board and protect your creatures. Stubborn Denial provides additional protection, while Lightning Bolt and Consign to Memory offer removal options. The deck also includes Scion of Draco for late-game power and versatility. The goal is to quickly build a board presence and close out the game before the opponent can respond effectively.

The main concept of this deck is to control the opponent's board using powerful eldritch creatures and spells, while utilizing the UrzaTron lands to quickly ramp up mana for big plays. The deck aims to disrupt the opponent's strategy with cards like Karn, the Great Creator and Thought-Knot Seer, while also utilizing Ugin, Eye of the Storms for removal and card advantage. The inclusion of cards like Devourer of Destiny and Glaring Fleshraker helps to apply pressure on the opponent, making it difficult for them to recover. The deck focuses on outlasting the opponent and overwhelming them with powerful threats.
